intro.uz: Slow Burn — energiya haqida: tez yugurib charchash emas, sekin va barqaror surʼatda uzoq davom etish. Stu Mittleman asta yugurib, kun boʻyi baquvvat qoladi. Bu haftaning ikki grammatik mavzusi shu gʻoyaga mos: present perfect (so far / up to now / yet — shu paytgacha nima qilib ulgurgansiz) va jarayon ravishlari (gradually / steadily / increasingly / little by little). Tiffani haqidagi POV hikoyada ikkalasini ham eshitasiz.
intro.en: Slow Burn is about energy — not sprinting and burning out, but going slow and steady so you can last. Stu Mittleman runs slowly and stays energetic all day. This week's two grammar topics fit that idea: the present perfect (so far / up to now / yet — what you have achieved up to now) and process adverbs (gradually / steadily / increasingly / little by little). You'll hear both in the POV story about Tiffany.

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida “What have you achieved so far?” savoliga toʻxtamasdan javob bera olasiz (I have … so far; I haven't … yet) va bir jarayonning bosqichma-bosqich oʻzgarishini jarayon ravishlari bilan tasvirlab bera olasiz (gradually / steadily / increasingly).
canDo.en: By the end you can answer “What have you achieved so far?” out loud without pausing (I have … so far; I haven't … yet), and describe how a process changes step by step using process adverbs (gradually / steadily / increasingly).

grammar.titleUz: Present perfect (sodda) — B1 boʻsagʻasi: so far / up to now / yet
grammar.titleEn: Present perfect — so far / up to now / yet
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 1 & 2 — say what you have done up to now
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1: I can say what I have and haven't done so far (so far / up to now / yet)
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tili buni koʻpincha oddiy oʻtgan zamon bilan aytadi: “Uch bob oʻqidim.” Ingliz tilida esa “shu paytgacha, natija hozir ham amalda” maʼnosi boʻlsa — present perfect kerak: “I have read three chapters so far.” Oʻzbekcha “hali …ganim yoʻq” = ingliz tilida “haven't … yet”.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ I didn't finish it yet. Did you eat yet? ✅ I haven't finished it yet. Have you eaten yet? “yet” (hali) savol va inkorda present perfect bilan keladi, oddiy oʻtgan zamon bilan emas.

examples.en: So far, I have read three chapters.
Up to now, she has saved half the money.
She hasn't found a purple cat yet.
examples.uz: U hali binafsha mushuk topgani yoʻq.

exercises.prompt: So far, I ___ finished two books this month.
exercises.hintUz: so far → shu paytgacha → present perfect (have + V3)

exercises.prompt: Up to now, we ___ won two races.
exercises.hintUz: we → have; “up to now” present perfectni talab qiladi

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting (ovoz chiqarib): “So far this year, I have … . But I haven't … yet.”

grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 2 — describe how something changed over time
grammar.cefrCanDo: B1→B2: I can describe a gradual trend (gradually / steadily / increasingly)
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbekchada “asta-sekin”, “tobora”, “oz-ozdan” mustaqil soʻzlar boʻlib, jumlaning turli joyida kelaveradi. Ingliz tilida esa koʻpi sifat + -ly shaklidagi ravishga aylanadi (gradual → gradually, steady → steadily) va oʻrni ancha qatʼiy: odatda asosiy feʼldan oldin yoki jumla boshida.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ It is becoming increasing difficult. She saved money gradual. ✅ It is becoming increasingly difficult. She saved money gradually. Feʼl yoki sifatni tasvirlaganda ravish kerak: sifat + -ly (increasingly, gradually).

examples.en: He runs steadily, never too fast.
examples.uz: U bir maromda yuguradi, hech qachon juda tez emas.

examples.en: Little by little, she saved the money.

exercises.prompt: My English has ___ improved this year.
___ by little, Tiffany saved a fortune.

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting: “Little by little, my … is getting better. It has gradually … .”
exercises.hintUz: ravish (gradually/steadily) asosiy feʼldan oldin; present perfect bilan have/has + ravish + V3

vocab.uz: bor kuch bilan juda tez yugurmoq
vocab.defEn: to run as fast as you possibly can for a short distance
vocab.example: You cannot sprint a marathon; you must go slowly.

vocab.uz: bilan uygʻun, hamohang; …ni his qilib turgan
vocab.defEn: in harmony or connection with something
vocab.example: Stay in tune with your body when you exercise.

vocab.defEn: for a period of time with no fixed end
vocab.example: You want energy that lasts indefinitely.

vocab.defEn: to make or find time out of a busy schedule
vocab.example: Try to carve out an hour for yourself every day.

vocab.defEn: to go at a speed you can maintain for a long time
vocab.example: In a long race you have to pace yourself.

vocab.defEn: to set on fire; to spark or start something
to change something completely into another thing
vocab.example: He wants to transform movement into personal power.

vocab.defEn: to give something up or let it go
vocab.example: She relinquished her dream of a purple cat.

vocab.uz: (vaqt yoki pul) ajratib qoʻymoq, saqlab qoʻymoq
vocab.defEn: to save or reserve something for later use
vocab.example: He sets aside two hours a day for running.

vocab.defEn: to set something aside for a specific purpose
vocab.example: She allocated all her savings to one goal.

englishpod.title: The Office – Interview Skills: Talking About Work
englishpod.titleUz: Ofis — ish suhbati: tajribangiz haqida gapirish

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda Rebekka ish suhbatida karerasini bosqichma-bosqich tasvirlaydi: amaliyotchilikdan boshlab, tajribasini yillar davomida asta-sekin oshirib boradi. Slow Burn gʻoyasiga mos: katta natija sekin, ammo barqaror qadamlar bilan quriladi. Sizningcha, Rebekka bu ishga qabul qilinadimi?
warmup.en: In this dialogue Rebecca describes her career step by step at a job interview: she started as an intern and built her experience gradually over the years. It fits the Slow Burn idea — big results are built with slow, steady steps. Do you think Rebecca will get the job?

keyVocab.defEn: to turn out or finally be in a certain situation
to succeed in doing something difficult
a move to a lower or less important position
chances or possibilities for success in the future
very interested in or eager to do something
a trainee who works, often unpaid, to gain experience

quiz.q: How many people aged between 40 and 60 do less than ten minutes brisk walking every month?
quiz.qUz: 40 va 60 yosh orasidagi qancha odam oyiga oʻn daqiqadan kam tez yuradi?
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: c) 40%. Neil 4% dedi va xato qildi — chunki oʻn daqiqa juda oz vaqtdek tuyuldi. Aslida Buyuk Britaniyada 40 dan 60 yoshgacha boʻlganlarning 40% i oyiga oʻn daqiqadan kam tez yuradi (Public Health England maʼlumoti).

vocab.uz: jadal, kuch talab qiladigan (koʻp energiya sarflaydigan)
vocab.defEn: using a lot of energy and effort
to walk slowly in a relaxed way

vocab.uz: (biror narsani) boshidanoq kiritmoq, hayotga qoʻshib olmoq
vocab.defEn: to include something as a regular part of a plan or routine
gradually increasing in size or amount
involving a lot of sitting and little physical activity

funEnglish.title: The first 20 hours -- how to learn anything | Josh Kaufman | TEDxCSU

speakingPrompt.en: Think about a goal you are working towards slowly — fitness, English, saving money. What have you achieved so far, and what haven't you done yet? How has your progress changed over time — gradually, steadily, increasingly?
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: present perfect (I have … so far; I haven't … yet) va jarayon ravishlari (gradually / steadily / increasingly / little by little).
